title: Inclusive Credit Risk Analytics Bolivia
problem: Credit and inclusion teams need a clear view of portfolio risk, inclusion indicators, stress signals and executive KPIs.
data: Public-facing aggregate analytical outputs and privacy-reviewed documentation.
analytical_workflow: Portfolio diagnostics, KPI design, risk segmentation, forecasting/stress communication, validation and public reporting.
key_findings: Demonstrates credit portfolio monitoring and financial-inclusion analytics through executive reporting rather than raw-data exposure.
limitations: Power BI is BUILD PACKAGE READY as a documented build package; it is not presented as a deployed public dashboard.

title: Poverty, Informality and Social Protection in LAC
problem: Policy and development teams need comparable country-year indicators linking poverty, labor informality and social protection coverage.
data: Public country-year analytical products, validation outputs, figures, tables and dashboard artifacts.
analytical_workflow: Multi-source indicator integration, country-year panel construction, missingness review, validation and dashboard communication.
key_findings: Shows policy-oriented data integration and development analytics with transparent validation and public navigation.
limitations: Tableau is BUILD PACKAGE READY as a documented build package; it is not presented as a deployed public dashboard.

title: Economic Complexity and Structural Transformation in LAC
problem: Research and policy audiences need to understand productive capabilities, diversification constraints and Bolivia's feasible opportunity space.
data: Public derived country-product-year outputs, Product Space diagnostics, validation files, paper, policy brief and DOI metadata.
analytical_workflow: RCA, ECI, PCI, Product Space proximity, opportunity screening, data-quality validation, paper and policy communication.
key_findings: Demonstrates advanced applied economics, network analytics and cautious policy interpretation using reproducible public outputs.
limitations: Opportunity scores are screening tools, not investment recommendations; fixed-effects estimates are observational associations.
